subject: Re: Subtomograms constantly coming out black
body:
Hi Dimitry
The new build fixed the bug, subtomograms look fine now. 
Thank you!
Nadav

On Tuesday, September 22, 2020 at 4:41:01 PM UTC+3 Nadav Elad wrote:

> Hi Dimitry
> Indeed, I tried reconstructing subtomograms from a data set collected with 
> no phase plate, so without using the phase shift option, and the density is 
> fine. 
> I tried using the new build without much success, and eventually realized 
> that the antivirus was identifying 3 files in the zip folder as a threat 
> and deleting them (including the warp.exe). Any idea why? I did not have 
> this problem with previous builds. 
> I contacted our IT and they are trying to address this issue as well.  
> Best
> Nadav
>
> On Tuesday, September 22, 2020 at 12:22:29 AM UTC+3 teg...@gmail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Nadav,
>>
>> sorry, there was a bug in the estimation of the aliasing-free box size 
>> that only manifested itself with phase shift != 0. This new build 
>> <http://deployment.warpem.com/nightly/Warp_20200921.zip> should fix this.
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>> On Wednesday, September 16, 2020 at 3:35:55 PM UTC+2 nada...@gmail.com 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Dimitry
>>>
>>> Thank you for the reply.
>>>
>>> I tried the 50 pixel box (and other sizes) with large and small pixel 
>>> sizes. The data was taken at 1.1 A/pixel and I tried going from 1.1 to 11 
>>> A/pixel. Tried also the nightly build you mentioned, but unfortunately it 
>>> did not solve this problem. 
>>> This is phase plate data with about 400nm measured defocus. Could it be 
>>> a normalization issue?
>>> I have another data set that was taken without phase plate, I will try 
>>> to process it and see if it makes a difference.
>>>
>>> Best
>>> Nadav 
>>>
>>> On Wednesday, September 16, 2020 at 1:14:06 AM UTC+3 teg...@gmail.com 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Nadav,
>>>>
>>>> when you say "small (50 pixel) boxes", do you mean same pixel size but 
>>>> smaller box, or same physical box size but larger pixels? Have you tried 
>>>> the nightly build 
>>>> <http://deployment.warpem.com/nightly/Warp_20200901.zip>? 1.0.9 
>>>> introduced a new mechanism to avoid CTF aliasing in sub-tomo reconstruction 
>>>> which will pad the box to an aliasing-free size (which depends on defocus 
>>>> and pixel size), reconstruct, and crop to the selected box size. The 
>>>> aliasing-free size can get really large. The updated version puts an upper 
>>>> limit on that at the expense of possible aliasing if you get closer to the 
>>>> Nyquist limit.
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>> Dimitry
>>>>
>>>> On Monday, September 14, 2020 at 8:06:29 AM UTC+2 nada...@gmail.com 
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi,
>>>>> My reconstructed subtomograms are constantly coming out black, i.e 
>>>>> with voxel values of zero. Would appreciate any idea how to solve this.
>>>>>
>>>>> I am running Warp on a workstation with Windows 10 and 4 Nvidia 1080 
>>>>> Ti GPU cards, so I don't see a memory issue. Reconstructing small (50 
>>>>> pixel) boxes does not help. The tomograms are of purified ribosomes, 
>>>>> collected using SerialEM with phase plate. Movies were motion corrected and 
>>>>> summed in Warp, followed by alignments of the tilts using IMOD, as 
>>>>> described in the tutorial. The tomograms reconstruct nicely in Warp and I 
>>>>> used template matching to locate the particles, which seem to give nice 
>>>>> peaks, and the resulting star file looks OK. I tried all the options in the 
>>>>> subtomogram reconstruction menu, but subtomos and 3D CTFs are always black. 
>>>>> Image series option results are fine, containing proper density.
>>>>>
>>>>> There was a suggestion in the forum to change the regional settings of 
>>>>> Windows 10, but it did not help here.
>>>>>
>>>>> I will be happy to share any file related to this project.
>>>>>
>>>>> Thank you
>>>>> Nadav
>>>>>
>>>>
